function Get-DependencyLicense {
    <#
    .SYNOPSIS
        Looks up the license for a dependency by name.
    .DESCRIPTION
        Uses a hashtable as a mock license database. Returns the license string
        if found, or "Unknown" if the package is not in the database.
        In production, this would call a registry API (npm, PyPI, etc.).
    #>
    [CmdletBinding()]
    param(
        [Parameter(Mandatory)]
        [string]$Name,

        [Parameter(Mandatory)]
        [hashtable]$LicenseDatabase
    )

    if ($LicenseDatabase.ContainsKey($Name)) {
        return $LicenseDatabase[$Name]
    }
    return "Unknown"
}

    <#
    .SYNOPSIS
        Looks up the license for a dependency by name.
    .DESCRIPTION
        Uses a hashtable as a mock license database. Returns the license string
        if found, or "Unknown" if the package is not in the database.
        In production, this would call a registry API (npm, PyPI, etc.).
    #>
    [CmdletBinding()]
    param(
        [Parameter(Mandatory)]
        [string]$Name,

        [Parameter(Mandatory)]
        [hashtable]$LicenseDatabase
    )

    if ($LicenseDatabase.ContainsKey($Name)) {
        return $LicenseDatabase[$Name]
    }
    return "Unknown"
}

function New-ComplianceReport {
    <#
    .SYNOPSIS
        Generates a full compliance report for all dependencies in a manifest.
    .DESCRIPTION
        Orchestrates: parse manifest -> look up each license -> check compliance.
        Returns a report object with Entries (per-dependency details) and Summary counts.
    #>
    [CmdletBinding()]
    param(
        [Parameter(Mandatory)]
        [string]$ManifestPath,

        [Parameter(Mandatory)]
        [hashtable]$LicenseDatabase,

        [Parameter(Mandatory)]
        [hashtable]$Config
    )

    $dependencies = Get-Dependencies -Path $ManifestPath
    $entries = @()

    foreach ($dep in $dependencies) {
        $license = Get-DependencyLicense -Name $dep.Name -LicenseDatabase $LicenseDatabase
        $status = Test-LicenseCompliance -License $license -Config $Config

        $entries += [PSCustomObject]@{
            Name    = $dep.Name
            Version = $dep.Version
            License = $license
            Status  = $status
        }
    }

    # Compute summary counts
    $approved = @($entries | Where-Object { $_.Status -eq "Approved" }).Count
    $denied   = @($entries | Where-Object { $_.Status -eq "Denied" }).Count
    $unknown  = @($entries | Where-Object { $_.Status -eq "Unknown" }).Count

    return [PSCustomObject]@{
        Entries = $entries
        Summary = [PSCustomObject]@{
            Total    = $entries.Count
            Approved = $approved
            Denied   = $denied
            Unknown  = $unknown
        }
    }
}

1	# DependencyLicenseChecker.ps1
2	# Parses dependency manifests, checks licenses against allow/deny lists,
3	# and generates a compliance report.
4	
5	function Get-Dependencies {
6	    <#
7	    .SYNOPSIS
8	        Parses a dependency manifest file and returns a list of dependencies.
9	    .DESCRIPTION
10	        Supports package.json (npm) and requirements.txt (Python).
11	        Returns objects with Name and Version properties.
12	    #>
13	    [CmdletBinding()]
14	    param(
15	        [Parameter(Mandatory)]
16	        [string]$Path
17	    )
18	
19	    if (-not (Test-Path $Path)) {
20	        throw "Manifest file not found: $Path"
21	    }
22	
23	    $fileName = [System.IO.Path]::GetFileName($Path)
24	    $results = @()
25	
26	    switch ($fileName) {
27	        "package.json" {
28	            $pkg = Get-Content -Path $Path -Raw | ConvertFrom-Json
29	            # Collect both dependencies and devDependencies
30	            foreach ($section in @('dependencies', 'devDependencies')) {
31	                if ($pkg.PSObject.Properties[$section]) {
32	                    $deps = $pkg.$section
33	                    foreach ($prop in $deps.PSObject.Properties) {
34	                        $results += [PSCustomObject]@{
35	                            Name    = $prop.Name
36	                            Version = $prop.Value
37	                        }
38	                    }
39	                }
40	            }
41	        }
42	        "requirements.txt" {
43	            # Parse pip requirements: lines like "package==1.0" or "package>=1.0"
44	            $lines = Get-Content -Path $Path
45	            foreach ($line in $lines) {
46	                $trimmed = $line.Trim()
47	                # Skip blank lines and comments
48	                if ($trimmed -eq '' -or $trimmed.StartsWith('#')) { continue }
49	                # Split on version specifiers (==, >=, <=, ~=, !=, >, <)
50	                if ($trimmed -match '^([A-Za-z0-9_.-]+)\s*[><=!~]=?\s*(.+)$') {
51	                    $results += [PSCustomObject]@{
52	                        Name    = $Matches[1]
53	                        Version = $Matches[2].Trim()
54	                    }
55	                } else {
56	                    # Package with no version pinned
57	                    $results += [PSCustomObject]@{
58	                        Name    = $trimmed
59	                        Version = "*"
60	                    }
61	                }
62	            }
63	        }
64	        default {
65	            throw "Unsupported manifest format: $fileName"
66	        }
67	    }
68	
69	    return $results
70	}
71	
72	function Get-DependencyLicense {
73	    <#
74	    .SYNOPSIS
75	        Looks up the license for a dependency by name.
76	    .DESCRIPTION
77	        Uses a hashtable as a mock license database. Returns the license string
78	        if found, or "Unknown" if the package is not in the database.
79	        In production, this would call a registry API (npm, PyPI, etc.).
80	    #>
81	    [CmdletBinding()]
82	    param(
83	        [Parameter(Mandatory)]
84	        [string]$Name,
85	
86	        [Parameter(Mandatory)]
87	        [hashtable]$LicenseDatabase
88	    )
89	
90	    if ($LicenseDatabase.ContainsKey($Name)) {
91	        return $LicenseDatabase[$Name]
92	    }
93	    return "Unknown"
94	}
95	
96	function Test-LicenseCompliance {
97	    <#
98	    .SYNOPSIS
99	        Checks a license against allow/deny lists and returns its compliance status.
100	    .DESCRIPTION
101	        Returns "Denied" if on the deny list (deny takes precedence),
102	        "Approved" if on the allow list, or "Unknown" otherwise.
103	    #>
104	    [CmdletBinding()]
105	    param(
106	        [Parameter(Mandatory)]
107	        [string]$License,
108	
109	        [Parameter(Mandatory)]
110	        [hashtable]$Config
111	    )
112	
113	    # Deny list takes precedence — safety first
114	    if ($Config.DenyList -contains $License) {
115	        return "Denied"
116	    }
117	    if ($Config.AllowList -contains $License) {
118	        return "Approved"
119	    }
120	    return "Unknown"
121	}
122	
123	function New-ComplianceReport {
124	    <#
125	    .SYNOPSIS
126	        Generates a full compliance report for all dependencies in a manifest.
127	    .DESCRIPTION
128	        Orchestrates: parse manifest -> look up each license -> check compliance.
129	        Returns a report object with Entries (per-dependency details) and Summary counts.
130	    #>
131	    [CmdletBinding()]
132	    param(
133	        [Parameter(Mandatory)]
134	        [string]$ManifestPath,
135	
136	        [Parameter(Mandatory)]
137	        [hashtable]$LicenseDatabase,
138	
139	        [Parameter(Mandatory)]
140	        [hashtable]$Config
141	    )
142	
143	    $dependencies = Get-Dependencies -Path $ManifestPath
144	    $entries = @()
145	
146	    foreach ($dep in $dependencies) {
147	        $license = Get-DependencyLicense -Name $dep.Name -LicenseDatabase $LicenseDatabase
148	        $status = Test-LicenseCompliance -License $license -Config $Config
149	
150	        $entries += [PSCustomObject]@{
151	            Name    = $dep.Name
152	            Version = $dep.Version
153	            License = $license
154	            Status  = $status
155	        }
156	    }
157	
158	    # Compute summary counts
159	    $approved = @($entries | Where-Object { $_.Status -eq "Approved" }).Count
160	    $denied   = @($entries | Where-Object { $_.Status -eq "Denied" }).Count
161	    $unknown  = @($entries | Where-Object { $_.Status -eq "Unknown" }).Count
162	
163	    return [PSCustomObject]@{
164	        Entries = $entries
165	        Summary = [PSCustomObject]@{
166	            Total    = $entries.Count
167	            Approved = $approved
168	            Denied   = $denied
169	            Unknown  = $unknown
170	        }
171	    }
172	}
173	
174	function Import-LicenseConfig {
175	    <#
176	    .SYNOPSIS
177	        Loads license allow/deny list configuration from a JSON file.
178	    .DESCRIPTION
179	        Expects a JSON file with "allowList" and "denyList" arrays.
180	        Returns a hashtable with AllowList and DenyList keys.
181	    #>
182	    [CmdletBinding()]
183	    param(
184	        [Parameter(Mandatory)]
185	        [string]$Path
186	    )
187	
188	    if (-not (Test-Path $Path)) {
189	        throw "Config file not found: $Path"
190	    }
191	
192	    $raw = Get-Content -Path $Path -Raw | ConvertFrom-Json
193	    return @{
194	        AllowList = @($raw.allowList)
195	        DenyList  = @($raw.denyList)
196	    }
197	}
198	
199	function Format-ComplianceReport {
200	    <#
201	    .SYNOPSIS
202	        Formats a compliance report object as a human-readable text string.
203	    #>
204	    [CmdletBinding()]
205	    param(
206	        [Parameter(Mandatory)]
207	        [PSCustomObject]$Report
208	    )
209	
210	    $sb = [System.Text.StringBuilder]::new()
211	    [void]$sb.AppendLine("=== Dependency License Compliance Report ===")
212	    [void]$sb.AppendLine()
213	
214	    foreach ($entry in $Report.Entries) {
215	        $statusTag = switch ($entry.Status) {
216	            "Approved" { "[APPROVED]" }
217	            "Denied"   { "[DENIED]"   }
218	            default    { "[UNKNOWN]"  }
219	        }
220	        [void]$sb.AppendLine("  $statusTag $($entry.Name)@$($entry.Version) - License: $($entry.License)")
221	    }
222	
223	    [void]$sb.AppendLine()
224	    [void]$sb.AppendLine("--- Summary ---")
225	    [void]$sb.AppendLine("  Total: $($Report.Summary.Total) | Approved: $($Report.Summary.Approved) | Denied: $($Report.Summary.Denied) | Unknown: $($Report.Summary.Unknown)")
226	
227	    return $sb.ToString()
228	}

  - Mock license lookups (known + unknown packages)
  - Compliance logic (approved, denied, unknown, deny-precedence)
  - Integration report generation (both manifest types, summary counts)
  - Config file loading
  - Text report formatting
